free_text: Cross sections for 3He interaction with 93Nb nuclei:
experimental data and possibilities of their
description
English translation
A detailed
description of the equipment used in this experiment to
irradiate the targets and to measure the gamma-spectra,
as well as the formulas for calculating the reaction
cross sections from the induced-activity values.
Pre-equilibrium emission and evaporation from
compound nuclei based on ALICE-85 code.
Aluminum foils calibrated in thickness served to
reduce gradually the energy of incident 3He ions.
The stacks were disposed in Faraday's cup
Two stacks have been used.
Three runs of measurements with different
time intervals were made.
Ultrapure germanium. The energy resolution
of this detector at Egamma = 1.3 MeV was 2.4 keV.
Errors in the cross sections comprise both
statistical and systematic uncertainties of the
experiment.
This uncertainty does not include:
Beam current uncertainty and
* decay data;
* systematic uncertainty of energies (1.0-1.5 MeV).
Needed corrections for secondary neutrons influence
and secondary electron yield from Faraday's cup were
included.
Table 3
Reaction lines are corrected
English translation of 'YF' was added
SD:DECAY-DATA corrected in Subents
016,018, 032. Data were checked, title and author's
list corrected according to PAN,59,2041,1996, except
last word in title: description -> prediction
(compiler believes that this is mistake in
translation). Small corrections in several Subents.
SAMPLE moved to Subents 002-036. ERR-T -> DATA-ERR in
Subents 002-036. MONITOR, REL-REF were added.
ANALYSIS (AREA) deleted (not relevant).
SD: Correction in several Subents.
Independent
Stacks of Al, Co and chemically pure Nb were used;
the foils were collect in stack,the thicknesses
of Nb was 30-23 mg/cm**2, of Al 52-12 mg/cm**2, of
Co 27-23 mg/cm**2. Irradiation time for activities
was 15. hr, beam flux=1.44 x 10**15 He-3 ions.
Cobalt targets in the long-lived assembly were
irradiated to measure the cross sections for reactions
on Co-59 nuclei.
Independent
Stacks of Al and chemically pure Nb were used;
the foils were collect in stack, the thicknesses
of Nb was 25-20 mg/cm**2, of Al 52-12 mg/cm**2.
Irradiation time for short-lived activities was 1.2 hr,
beam flux=4.9 x 10**13 He-3 ions.
